Frost-Covered Tome [Melee Version]  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE SECONDARY
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +3 STA: +3 HP: +30
SV FIRE: +15
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Lore Item No Trade Placeable

SV FIRE: +15

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 35
[Drops | Comments ]

This item is found on creatures.

NPC Name

loc
QuoteReply
# Nov 28 2003 at 7:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775246591
128 posts
loc of spawn for Narmak Berreka who drops this is on 4th floor ToFS at p887, p83, p176

Comming back
QuoteReply
# Nov 01 2002 at 5:02 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758106980
222 posts
My 45 troll SK and my brother's 51 dwarf cleric where crawling our way through the 4th floor when we came upon this guy - would have been OK too, except it turned into add central on us and he was low mana at the time to boot - didn't know at the time that this guy was carrying such a nice peice - when I saw the book I figured my bro was gonna get something nice.

Sigh, ah well - he wants a ring from the 5th floor, so we're comming back eventually.

Naggy raid usefulness
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2002 at 11:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776825188
11 posts
Only use I can really see this for, for higher lvl ppl, is Naggy raids...and that is just what I intend to use it for. 15svfire in range slot is quite nice. :P


Edited, Wed Mar 13 11:38:37 2002
▲ Page top
quick swiching
QuoteReply
# Sep 26 2001 at 4:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592251429273
1 post
I think I have an easy way to switch, I use it all the time. I just made "hot buttons" out of my range slot and my first general slots. I put them at the bottom of my hot button list.

Normally, I leave the book in the range slot at all times. When I want to shoot my bow, I just pick it up from the hot button, click once on the range slot hot button, fire the bow (another hot button, of course), click again to swap back and then return the bow to the gerneral slot.

This may sound a bit complex, but it couldn't be easier. Note that you never actually have to put the book into the general slot, just hold it with cursor as you hit the "range attack" key. Just click-click, fire, click-click.

Hope this is useful,

- Qweller

▲ Page top
RE: quick swiching
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2002 at 5:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753208691
View User Forum
55 posts
Aye, this is a good strategy for medium and small races that can't slam. As a warrior I us dual weild, prior to level 55 I had no way to interupt mob's casting so I kept a sheil in an open inv slot. When I say "MobX begins casting.." I swapped the sheid into 2nd melee, hit bash, and swap the 1h weapon back in. Worked great. At first it's awkward but you get the hang of it. At level 55, warrior's kick will stun, no need for sheild anymore.

Balker Neehigh
Warlord
The Commune
Xegony
▲ Page top
RE: quick swiching
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 2:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591758352509
100 posts
not complex at all actually if one actually tries it. i do the same on my bard for instruments except i have 3 slots. really good for people who do not have alot of right click item macros or spell macros
▲ Page top
Two of These
QuoteReply
# Sep 14 2001 at 9:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591766766868
4 posts
There are two of these items. I believe one is from Frozen Shadow and the other from Lord Bob(as he is known because his name is too hard)

One is for warriors and the other is for casters.
The casters one is not no drop either which is cool.

CLR WIZ NEC MAG ENC

▲ Page top
Range Slot
QuoteReply
# Apr 15 2001 at 5:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756602073
9 posts
This item can be equipped in the range slot. I have had one for about two months, and switch it out with my bow depending on if I am pulling or assisting. Just make sure if you do this that you DO NOT accidentally throw this at the mob when pulling! hehe I usually get rid of my 'range attack' button when I equip this just to be safe, since this is far too sweet of an item to risk losing because of a stupid mistake! Have fun and safe hunting!

Drakokorin Glasjaaw
47th Warrior
Circle of Legends
"If it doesn't con red, and looks at ya funny...Kill it!"
▲ Page top
RE: Range Slot
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2002 at 6:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1670199487804
Scholar
8,619 posts
True. Only items with a range stat, Range: xx, can be thrown - everything else just sits there. So DO remove your range attack key when holding a Runed Bone Fork, for this - it doesn't matter
▲ Page top
RE: Range Slot
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 11:14 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have one of these. Doesnt get thrown when ya hit the range button. It either does nothing or makes it look like ya holding this in second hand and nothing in primary. Does both im not saying it should i saying it DOES.

▲ Page top
More info
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2001 at 10:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245817043
4 posts
Narmak Barreka is a random spawn off all the mobs on level four. In other words, any mob can be his PH. Usually, if you're camping Cara's room, you'll pull him at least once.

This is an *awsome* item for pallys/rangers/SKs because they dont need a bow to pull. Its great for warriors on a naggy raid, or when they arent pulling.

By the way, Narmak IS a shadowknight, so he does have an HT. He does also drop a Sheild of Shadows, which is nice SK only sheild. Worth a lot. :)

Good luck to yall. :D

Delphi Aegis
▲ Page top
A ranger's POV
QuoteReply
# Dec 29 2000 at 9:04 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I recently acquired one of these and I'm not sure which I'd rather have in my range slot - this or my raincaller, which has stat bonuses of its own. Of course the fire resist is good for dragon raids and the like so I may switch between the two on occasion. I'm sure anyone else who has a magic bow of some sort would face the same dilemma.
▲ Page top
RE: A ranger's POV
QuoteReply
# Mar 03 2001 at 9:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754967810
29 posts
Another Ranger's PoV:
This would be an item mainly used as resist gear although for a Dragon raid a RBF is probably better, unless you have enough magic resist elsewhere. Under rare circumstances would I ever use this in place of a bow (i.e. I'm not pulling).

Ionas Bladesong
Tunare's Guards
E Marr
▲ Page top
Drops from...
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2000 at 7:52 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This item drops off a common named mob on lvl 4 of the Tower of Frozen Shadow. The mobs name is Narmek Berraks (or something along those lines) and is a Suit of Armor. Was blue at 39, fairly easy kill. There are usually shadows around him though. Everytime we killed him he dropped this tome. He never dropped anything else except coin. This may or may not be for a quest. The item can go in hand slot or ranged. Hope this helps.
▲ Page top
RE: Drops from...
QuoteReply
# Dec 18 2000 at 7:01 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Interesting...will meleers give up a second weapon or shield to look like they're holding a book, or will they give up their bows & arrows so they can have these bonuses? Looks like a lot of the loot from here is like that. Sweet, but has a little catch...

Once you're in close combat, equip the book in your range slot. I have a 25 warrior and only use arrows for pulling. It would be nice if there were an easier way to swap items than using the mouse.

I got one of these from ToFS. Normally, if I am pulling this stays in one of my little bags. If I am required to peel; I may swap it out with the bow.

All other times it stays there in my range slot as it is a very handy piece.
